title: tabernaemontana lorifera
intro: Tabernaemontana lorifera, a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly known as the "butterfly tree" due to its fragrant flowers that attract butterflies. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Tabernaemontana lorifera include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, as well as its use in treating conditions such as hypertension and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are taken to harness its medicinal properties.
